excerpt: கல்லும் உருகிடும் ஓர் நாள் கந்த நாமம் பஜித்தே விதி

sections.label: Pallavi
sections.ta:
கல்லும் உருகிடும் ஓர் நாள் கந்த நாமம் பஜித்தே விதி
வெல்லும் வழி அதுவென்று அறியாயோ மனிதா
sections.translit:
kallum urugidum Or nAL kandha nAmam bhajitthE vidhi
vellum vazhi adhuvendRu aRiyAyO manidhA
sections.en: Even a stone will melt one day, singing the glory of Skandha. Oh, human form, don’t you realize that singing His glory is the only way to overcome your fate?

sections.label: Anupallavi
sections.ta:
அல்லும் பகலும் அருணாசலன் குமரன் அவன் நினைவின்றியே
தில்லும் முல்லுமாய் திரிவதும் நிலை சரிவதுமேனோ
sections.translit:
allum pagalum arunAchalan kumaran avan ninaivindRiyE
thillum mullumAy thirivadhum nilai sarivadhumEnO
sections.en: Why do you then wander around day and night, without the thought of Arunachalan[1]’s son, thereby degrading yourself and losing your status?

sections.label: Charanam
sections.ta:
மெத்தையில் மெல்லிடையாரோடு அலைந்து சித்தில் சீரும் குலைந்து
சொத்தையெல்லாம் இழந்து வந்த பித்தில் நாராய் நலிந்து
நிலை குனிந்து மலை நின்று மடு நோக்கி தாவினான்
அலைவாயிறைவன் விரைந்தே வந்தவனைத் தாங்கினான் அது கண்ட
sections.translit:
metthaiyil mellidaiyArOdu alaindhu sitthil sIrum kulaindhu
sotthaiyellAm izhandhu vandha pitthil nArAy nalindhu
nilai kunindhu malai nindRu madu nOkki thAvinAn
alaivAyiRaivan viraindhE vandhavanaith thAnginAn adhu kanda
sections.en: A fellow spent all his time as a youth in bed with slender waisted women and gave up clear thought. He wasted all his wealth in petty pleasures and became near crazy. He ruined his physical health as well and became thin as a fiber. In his desperation, looking at the stage where now he would have to bend rather than stand tall, he decided to end his life by jumping off the cliff into an abyss. The Lord of Thirucheeralaivay[2] rushed to the spot on His peacock and received and supported him. The stone that witnessed this merciful act of Lord Muruga one day will melt, singing His glory. [1] Another name for Lord Siva. [2] A name for Thiruchendur given by Nakkeerar in his Thirumurugatruppadai. Author’s Note The charanam of this song references the life story of Saint Arunagirinathar. Czech Indologist Kamil Zvelebil places Arunagirinadhar’s time between 1370 and 1450 CE. Arunagirinadhar spent his youth pursuing a life of debauchery, spending all his wealth on women and sexual pleasure. At a point in life when he became poor and infected with a contagious disease, everyone left him, and he became depressed. One day he decided to commit suicide, tried to jump off the temple wall. Lord Muruga appeared at that point and offered his helping hand, enlightened him, and asked him to compose Thiruppugazh. Arunagirinadhar’s Thiruppugazh, which he sang upon enlightenment after seeing Lord Muruga, is the crown jewel of medieval Tamil literature. A short story of Arunagirinathar’s life is provided in the links below: Facts about Arunagirinathar Life Story of Arunagirinathar.pdf
